name: Vanilla Bean Caramels
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Firmly packed golden brown sugar
36 Pieces of cellophane or wax paper (each measuring 4-1/2 by 6 in)
recipeInstructions:
1. Line an 8-inch square baking pan with foil, covering the bottom and sides.
2. Brush generously with vegetable oil.
3. In a heavy 3-quart saucepan over medium heat, combine the brown sugar, granulated sugar, corn syrup, whole milk, condensed milk, cream, butter, vanilla bean, and salt.
4. Stir constantly until the sugar dissolves and the mixture comes to a boil.
5. Using a pastry brush dipped in water, brush down the sides of the pan to prevent sugar crystals from forming.
6. Raise the heat to medium-high and clip a candy thermometer onto the side of the pan.
7. Cook, stirring slowly but constantly, until the thermometer registers 240°F, about 10 minutes.
8. Remove the pan from the heat. Remove the vanilla bean and discard it.
9. Immediately pour the caramel into the prepared pan all at once; do not scrape the residue from the pan bottom.
10. Let the caramel cool completely, about two hours.
11. Coat a cutting board with vegetable oil. Turn out the cooled caramel onto the board and peel off the foil.
12. Oil a large knife and cut the caramel into strips about 1-1/2 inches wide.
13. Cut the strips crosswise into pieces 1 inch long, re-oiling the knife occasionally to prevent sticking.
14. Wrap each candy in a piece of clear cellophane or wax paper, twisting the ends.
15. Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 weeks.
Walnut lovers might want to stir 1-1/2 cups chopped walnuts into the caramel after removing the vanilla bean and stirring gently.
